shared_properties: Every state keeps Umbra as a shadow-dominant silhouette with a readable gold rim accent and no internal soft shading.
The authored frame baseline aligns to the same 32 px platform grid and 16:9 camera framing used everywhere in the package.
Primary palette metadata remains #2D1B4E, background #0D0B14, and accent #C9A84C while danger flashes stay inside specs.colors_hex.
differences: The atlas groups six gameplay states in one file so the animator can author consistent limb proportions across traversal and exposure transitions.
Run frames exaggerate forward claw sweep while idle frames stay compact to preserve puzzle readability in low-motion moments.
Exposure frames introduce #F5EFE0 fracture highlights and a sharper amber rim to communicate active light damage.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

reference_images.label: umbra_character_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra body proportions, head silhouette, claw shapes, and echo-friendly shadow anatomy.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load umbra_player_core_states.png during Loading and register one atlas layout that exposes idle, run, jump, fall, grab, and exposure state slices by named frame ranges before gameplay begins.
runtime_notes: Keep the gameplay collision body at the GDD hitbox of 28x44 px even though the authored sprite frame is larger for silhouette readability.
Switch state clusters from ECS movement and exposure systems rather than by filename so Bevy state transitions stay deterministic.
Anchor the feet to the same floor baseline used by the scale rig and ladder set so jump, land, and grab frames line up without per-state offsets.

context.usage: Displayed for traversal, grab interactions, exposure feedback, and scale-weight positioning across every floor.

silhouette_notes: The head crest, shoulder mass, and claw tips remain readable against both #0D0B14 and #F5EFE0 fields.
The grab pose keeps one arm clearly extended toward mirror-height props at 96 px authored frame scale.
Exposure frames add bright edge breakup without collapsing the core shadow silhouette.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 480x864 px for thirty 96x144 frames with no frame gutter.
The 28x44 px gameplay hitbox fits entirely inside every frame without clipping the head crest or foot contact pose.
Idle, run, grab, and exposure states remain distinguishable at 50% preview scale against both dark and bright test backdrops.
